data.title: XMax Engages Cloud Alliance for AWS AI Inference Platform in $400,000 Services Deal
data.description:
XMax entered into an AI Inference Platform Deployment and Service Agreement with Cloud Alliance, effective April 1, 2026. The $400,000 fixed-fee arrangement covers development, deployment, and necessary configuration of an AI inference platform in the company’s designated AWS environment. XMax will make a $200,000 non-refundable mobilization payment shortly after signing, with the remaining $200,000 due upon written acceptance. The project is expected to accelerate deployment of production-ready AI capabilities for an approved use case.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: AI Inference Platform Deployment and Service Agreement
  • Counterparty: Cloud Alliance
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 06 2026 / Apr 01 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Deploy AI inference platform on AWS for approved use case

Original SEC Filing: XMax Inc. [ XWIN ] - 8-K - Apr. 08, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Richardson Electronics reports Q3 net income $0.9M, revenue $55.5M; Board declares $0.06 dividend
data.description:
Richardson Electronics reported third-quarter fiscal 2026 net income of $0.9 million (diluted EPS $0.07) on net sales of $55.5 million, a 3.1% year‑over‑year increase. The company posted gross profit of $17.7 million (31.9% of net sales) and operating income of $1.5 million for the quarter. The board declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.06 per common share payable May 27, 2026.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: Net sales for Q3 FY2026 were $55.5 million, up 3.1% versus Q3 FY2025 ($53.8 million).
  • Gross profit and margin: Gross profit for Q3 FY2026 was $17.68 million, representing 31.9% of net sales.
  • Operating income: Operating income for the quarter was $1.5 million.
  • Net income and EPS: Net income was $0.9 million; diluted earnings per common share were $0.07 for the quarter.
  • Adjusted / Non‑GAAP metrics: EBITDA for Q3 FY2026 was $2.2 million; non‑GAAP net income per diluted share was $0.07 (adjusted to exclude a prior one‑time Healthcare asset sale loss).

Business Highlights

  • Segment momentum: PMT led sales growth with Q3 PMT net sales of $38.7 million, a 9.7% increase year‑over‑year driven by semiconductor wafer fab and RF/microwave products.
  • Backlog growth: Total backlog increased 11.4 sequentially to $151.2 million at February 28, 2026, reaching the highest level in nearly three years and driven primarily by PMT orders.
  • Segment mix shifts: Since the January 2025 Healthcare asset sale, Healthcare was consolidated into PMT; excluding Healthcare, PMT net sales rose 14.5% in the quarter.
  • Operational investments: Company increased headcount and technical hires, incurring higher salaries, incentives and related benefits to support growth and product development efforts.
  • Balance sheet and liquidity: Cash and cash equivalents were $29.5 million at quarter end and the company had no outstanding borrowing on its revolving credit facility with PNC Bank.

Original SEC Filing: RICHARDSON ELECTRONICS, LTD. [ RELL ] - 8-K - Apr. 08, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Pioneer Power Sol posts $27.6M revenue in 2025; Q4 revenue $5.6M, net loss $6.0M
data.description:
Pioneer Power Sol reported full-year 2025 revenue of $27.6 million, up 20.8% year-over-year and in line with company guidance, while reporting a net loss of $6.0 million for the year. Fourth-quarter 2025 revenue was $5.6 million with a net loss of $1.4 million and gross margin of 23.5% for the quarter. The company disclosed $15.0 million in cash at year-end and a backlog of $12.6 million as of December 31, 2025.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $27.6 million for the year ended December 31, 2025; $5.6 million for Q4 2025.
  • Gross profit: $3.4 million for full-year 2025 (12.4% gross margin); Q4 gross profit $1.3 million (23.5% gross margin).
  • Operating loss from continuing operations: $(6.6) million for full-year 2025; $(1.1) million for Q4 2025.
  • Net (loss) income: $(6.0) million for full-year 2025 (includes $449,000 from discontinued operations); Q4 net loss $(1.4) million (includes $(17,500) from discontinued operations).
  • Non-GAAP operating (loss) income from continuing operations: $(98,000) for 2025; $589,000 for Q4 2025 (non-GAAP excludes corporate overhead, R&D, D&A and non-recurring professional fees).

Business Highlights

  • Expanded product portfolio beyond mobile EV charging with the launch of the PRYMUS Mobile Distributed Energy Platform (designed for 1 MW–10 MW off-grid deployments) and the PowerCore Residential Prime Energy Platform targeting whole-home 24/7/365 resiliency with integrated high-speed charging.
  • Secured a strategic international agreement to scale the e-Boost mobile charging ecosystem globally using a franchise model and local partnerships to pursue capital-light expansion into international EV markets.
  • Maintained steady demand for e-Boost across core markets with an established leasing and service model generating recurring revenue and supporting the company’s distributed energy ecosystem.
  • Reported initial customer engagements for PRYMUS in Q1 2026 with shipments scheduled for 2027 and expected PowerCore shipments beginning in the second half of 2026, indicating progression from product launches toward commercial deployment.

Original SEC Filing: PIONEER POWER SOLUTIONS, INC. [ PPSI ] - 8-K - Apr. 08, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: FDA Lifts Partial Clinical Hold on MacroGenics' LINNET Study; Enrollment to Resume
data.description:
MacroGenics announced the FDA has removed the partial clinical hold on its Phase 2 LINNET study of lorigerlimab, allowing enrollment to resume.

Key Highlights:

  • FDA removed the partial clinical hold on the Phase 2 LINNET study of lorigerlimab, a PD-1/CTLA-4 bispecific DART molecule.
  • Previously enrolled participants may continue treatment; new participants will be enrolled under a revised protocol.
  • Revised protocol includes additional risk-mitigation measures for potential hematologic and cardiac toxicities.
  • LINNET evaluates single-agent lorigerlimab in up to ~60 patients with platinum-resistant ovarian or clear cell gynecologic cancers; 41 participants dosed to date.
  • Company remains on track to provide a mid-2026 clinical update on the program.

Original SEC Filing: MACROGENICS INC [ MGNX ] - 8-K - Apr. 08, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Dynatrace to Buy Telemetry Provider Bindplane
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Dynatrace agreed to acquire Bindplane, a telemetry-pipeline provider.

Telemetry, which shows what is happening inside digital systems, is in higher demand because of the rise of artificial intelligence, Dynatrace said Wednesday.

Bindplane's technology improves telemetry-data quality, reduces ingest costs and enhances compliance by removing or encrypting sensitive data.

The deal is expected to close later this month and isn't expected to have a material effect on Dynatrace's fiscal 2027 financial results.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Costco Reports 11% Growth in March Sales
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Costco reported $28.41 billion in net sales for the retail month of March, up 11% from a year earlier.

The wholesale retailer reported a 9.4% increase in comparable sales during the five weeks ended April 5, including an 8.7% increase in the U.S., an 10.7% growth in Canada, and 11.9% growth in other international markets.

Digitally enabled comparable sales rose 23% compared with the year-ago period.

Stripping out the impact of exchange rates and higher gas prices, comparable sales rose 6.2%.

The company said the retail month had one fewer shopping day compared with last year, negatively impacting total sales by about 1.5%.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: AI Data-Center Developer's Earnings Top Views. Why Applied Digital Stock Is Moving Higher. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Kit Norton

Applied Digital's fiscal 2026 third-quarter results broadly topped Wall Street estimates after the closing bell on Wednesday, as the company said it sees accelerating demand for AI data-center capacity.

The Dallas, Texas.,-based AI data-center developer announced late Wednesday adjusted earnings of 9 cents per share, well above Wall Street's consensus view of a 16-cent loss, according to FactSet. The company also reported revenue growing 139% to $126.64 million, topping expectations for sales totaling $75.5 million.

Applied Digital shares jumped 2.4% after the closing bell Wednesday. The stock rose 11% to $27.87 during regular trading, as many stocks rallied following news of a cease-fire in the Iran conflict. For comparison, the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite gained 2.8% on Wednesday.

Write to Kit Norton at kit.norton@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Garda Therapeutics to Acquire Assertio Holdings for $125.1M in Cash
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Assertio Holdings agreed to be acquired by Garda Therapeutics for $125.1 million in cash.

The pharmaceutical company also said it signed and closed an agreement to sell all non-Rolvedon assets to Cosette Pharmaceuticals for an up-front payment of $35 million, plus earnouts related to product milestones.

It said the Cosette transaction is reflected in the total cash consideration for the Garda acquisition, which represents a price of $18 per share.

Garda's price represents a roughly 35% premium to the company's stock price on March 20, and a 62% premium to the company's 60-day unaffected volume-weighted average price as of that day, Assertio said.

In addition to the cash payment, Garda will also issue a non-tradable contingent value right for potential future milestones of the company's Spirx drug.

Garda will acquire shares through a tender offer, Assertio said. Following the tender offer, Garda will acquire all non-tendered shares through a second-step merger at the same price of $18 a share.

The boards of directors of both companies have unanimously agreed to the transaction, which is expected to close in the second quarter.

Shares of Assertio fell slightly to $18.29 in after-hours trading Wednesday. The stock closed down 1% at $18.41, having doubled in value year to date.

Assertio Board Chair Heather Mason said the company evaluated multiple strategic options, including a sale, merger opportunities, or continuing as a standalone opportunity. It engaged more than 35 counterparties, including strategic and financial buyers, she said.

"Following this thorough process — and with the addition under the agreement for an incremental shop period to ensure maximum value — the board has determined that these transactions with Cosette and Garda provide the best outcome for our shareholders," Mason said.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Argan Approves $50M Increase to Share-Buyback Program
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Argan approved an increase of its share-buyback program to $200 million.

Alongside the increase from $150 million, the board also approved the buyback program's extension through Jan. 31, 2030.

The construction company also declared a quarterly dividend of 50 cents a share, which will be payable on April 30 to shareholders of record as of April 22.

The dividend is the same amount as the previous two quarters. It represents an annual payout of $2 a share and represents a yield of 0.3% based on Wednesday's closing price of $588.28.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Western Alliance Bancorporation Announces First Quarter 2026 Earnings Release Date, Conference Call and Webcast
data.description:
PHOENIX--(BUSINESS WIRE)--April 08, 2026--

Western Alliance Bancorporation announced today that it plans to release its first quarter 2026 financial results after the market closes on Tuesday, April 21, 2026. Kenneth A. Vecchione, President and CEO and Vishal Idnani, Chief Financial Officer, will host a conference call at 12:00 p.m. ET on Wednesday, April 22, 2026 to discuss the Company's performance.

Participants may access the call by dialing 1-888-596-4144 using the access code 9350603 or via live audio webcast using the website link: https://events.q4inc.com/attendee/403697580.

The webcast is also available through the Company's website at www.westernalliancebancorporation.com. Participants should log in at least 15 minutes early to receive instructions. The call will be recorded and made available for replay April 22(nd) after 3:00 p.m. ET until April 29(th) at 11:59 p.m. ET by dialing 1-800-770-2030 using the access code: 9350603.

About Western Alliance Bancorporation

Western Alliance Bancorporation is one of the country's top-performing banking companies. Its primary subsidiary, Western Alliance Bank, Member FDIC, is a leading national bank for business that puts customers first, delivering tailored business banking solutions and consumer products backed by outstanding, personalized service and specific expertise in more than 30 industries and sectors. With $90 billion in assets and offices nationwide, Western Alliance has ranked as a top U.S. bank by American Banker and Bank Director since 2016. In 2025, Western Alliance Bancorporation was #2 for Best CEO, Best CFO and Best Company Board of Directors on Extel's All-America Executive Team Midcap Banks list. For more information on offerings, subsidiaries and affiliates, visit www.westernalliancebank.com or follow Western Alliance Bank on LinkedIn.

data.title: ICE Canada Weekly Outlook: War News Driving Market
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--News of a tentative two-week ceasefire between the United States and Iran sent crude oil values sharply lower on April 8, with canola futures falling in sympathy. While the selloff broke the oilseed out of its nearby trading range, additional direction is expected to continue to come from developments in the Middle East.

May canola settled at C$704.90 per ton on April 8, falling out of the sideways trading range it had held for the previous three weeks.

From a technical standpoint, May canola had been creating a coiling pattern over the past month, with the break lower opening up significant room to the downside, said analyst Mike Jubinville.

Looking at a chart, the next support comes in at C$700 per ton and then around C$680.

However, seasonal price trends at this time of year generally point higher, said Jubinville.

While old crop canola supplies remain more than sufficient to meet demand, he added that reduced fertilizer applications due to high costs could be supportive in the longer term.

With the bearish technical signals on one side and supportive seasonals on the other, "the markets will just trade the war," added Western Producer markets desk analyst Bruce Burnett.

Source: Commodity News Service Canada, news@marketsfarm.com

data.title: Audited results of Invalda INVL Group for 2025
data.description:
Invalda INVL reported equity of EUR 256.2 million by the end of 2025, or EUR 21.25 per share. These figures were 22.1% and 21.8% higher, respectively, than a year ago, including the dividends paid out last year.

In 2025 Invalda INVL earned an audited net profit of EUR 47.1 million – 6.1% more compared to the previous year's profit of EUR 44.4 million. Following these strong results, the company's board will propose that shareholders approve a dividend distribution of nearly EUR 12.1 million, or EUR 1.0 per share. The final decision on dividends will be taken at the General Meeting of Shareholders on 30 April.

"We are living in a period when geopolitical and economic uncertainty has become a new reality. Therefore, consistent teamwork, the ability to adapt to ongoing changes and seize emerging opportunities, decision-making and execution, diversification and effective liquidity management are particularly important in achieving our goals of preserving and growing our clients' wealth, as well as increasing the value of the businesses and investments we manage," says Darius Šulnis, CEO of Invalda INVL.

According to D. Šulnis, the target return for 2025 was achieved for most client investments, although stagnation was felt in certain asset classes, such as renewable energy, forestry and agricultural land markets.

Strategic core business: asset management and family office services

The total value of client assets under management grew by 26.4% during the year, reaching 2.1 billion EUR at the end of December 2025. Invalda INVL's revenue from the management of assets entrusted by its clients totalled EUR 20.3 million in 2025, 44% more than in 2024. The profit of strategic core business of the group, including the company's own investments in managed products, amounted to EUR 8.8 million, compared with EUR 17.8 million profit in 2024.

One of the most significant events of the past year was the increased activity in the private equity sector. The INVL Private Equity Fund II was launched after raising a record amount of EUR 410 million. The fund has already completed two transactions – investing in Estonia's Eesti Keskkonnateenused and Poland's POLMED. Meanwhile, its predecessor, INVL Baltic Sea Growth Fund, was active in both directions: it completed its portfolio formation by acquiring Romania's Pehart Group and successfully exited its investments in InMedica Group in Lithuania and MBL in Denmark. Following these transactions, the fund has fully returned the capital invested and continues to increase the value of its remaining investments. The IT investment company INVL Technology, preparing to sell its portfolio of managed companies, demonstrated stable financial growth last year – its net asset value increased by 26% during the year to EUR 64.6 million, and net profit grew 1.6 times to EUR 13.3 million.

Total revenues across the Invalda INVL group’s portfolio companies of private equity funds exceeded EUR 1 billion in 2025, with EBITDA totalling EUR 250 million and combined 9 thousand employees at year-end.

The infrastructure sector, in which the group plans to be active, is a relevant area in the region. In 2025, the INVL Defence Infrastructure Fund I was established to invest in the development of part of the Rūdninkai training ground infrastructure to meet the needs of the German brigade and the Lithuanian Armed Forces.

INVL Bridge Finance, a private debt fund managed by the group, also ended 2025 with solid growth: its net asset value exceeded EUR 50 million, and the annual return to investors reached 9.2%.

The INVL Family Office, which operates in all three Baltic States, had a significantly increased flow of client investments in the past year. Clients invested in both local and global markets through managed funds, including feeder funds, and other investment products.

Equity Investments

Invalda INVL’s other equity investments, aside from the asset management, had a EUR 35.3 million impact on earnings in 2025.

In 2025, Artea bank continued to deploy a new banking platform and continued to implement other changes to strengthen the bank and meet the growing needs of its clients. The positive impact of Artea Bank, including dividends paid, to Invalda INVL's pre-tax profit amounted to EUR 23.5 million.

Meanwhile, maib, the largest Moldova's bank, continued to demonstrate record operating results, earning MDL 1.99 billion last year (equivalent to EUR 101.8 million) – 41% more than in 2024. The positive impact of maib to Invalda INVL's pre-tax profit reached EUR 6.4 million.

Litagra, the successfully operating food-supply chain business group with plans for further expansion, had a positive impact of EUR 5.5 million on Invalda INVL’s result for 2025. Having reached a stage of business maturity, Litagra began evaluating strategic alternatives and opportunities for further value creation. To assist with this process, Litagra has engaged the services of financial advisers Oaklins Lithuania. Various scenarios are being considered, including the potential sale of some or all businesses, expansion, or strategic partnerships. This is an analysis of possibilities and will not necessarily result in specific transactions.

"In 2026, we will pursue further strong organic growth of the Invalda INVL group's business, as well as technological and qualitative improvements to our operational activities. We are also prepared to expand the business by acquiring local or regional market participants," concludes Darius Šulnis.

Further information:

Darius Šulnis, CEO of Invalda INVL

Darius.Sulnis@invl.com

  • invaldainvl-2025-12-31-0-en
  • Invalda INVL Auditors Report EN 2025
  • Invalda INVL Group overview Y2025

data.title: Constellation Brands 4Q Revenue Down Despite Uptick in Beer Sales
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Constellation Brands logged lower revenue in its latest quarter, as a decline in wine and spirit sales offset a slight increase in beer sales.

The U.S. importer of Modelo and Corona beers on Wednesday posted a profit of $201.8 million, or $1.16 a share, compared with a loss of $375.3 million, or $2.09 a share, a year earlier.

Adjusted earnings per share were $1.90, compared with estimates of $1.71 a share according to analysts polled by FactSet.

Revenue slid 11% to $1.92 billion, compared with analyst estimates of $1.88 billion.

Beer sales increased 1% to $1.73 billion, driven by higher shipments and prices. Wine and spirits sales slid 58%, to $194.2 million on lower shipments due to divestitures, changes in distributor contractual obligations and pricing actions in select brands, the company said.

Constellation said it is encouraged by the momentum across both businesses in the quarter, but expects the environment to remain dynamic in the year ahead given an evolving socioeconomic backdrop. The company also withdrew the fiscal 2028 outlook it provided last year.

The company has previously noted that socioeconomic headwinds, especially among Hispanic consumers, have dented sales.

"We expect consumers will continue to navigate a shifting macroeconomic environment," Chief Executive Bill Newlands said.

For the fiscal year ahead, the company guided for adjusted earnings per share of $11.20 to $11.90. Analysts project adjusted earnings per share of $12.38 for the year.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: STZ Stock Drops After-Hours On Disappointing Q4 Earnings, FY27 Guidance
data.description:
Shares of Constellation Brands, Inc. (STZ) fell 1% in extended trading on Wednesday after the company’s guidance for fiscal year 2027 (FY27) disappointed investors.

The company now sees comparable FY27 earnings per share of $11.20-$11.90, below Wall Street estimates of $12.36, according to data from Fiscal.ai.

“Looking ahead to fiscal 2027, while we are encouraged by the momentum displayed during the fourth quarter across our Beer and Wine & Spirits businesses, we expect the operating environment to remain dynamic given the evolving socioeconomic backdrop and limited near-term visibility,” the company said.

For Q4, the company reported earnings per share of $1.16, compared to the loss of $2.09 reported in the corresponding quarter of last year, but below an analyst estimate of $1.7. Revenue for the quarter came in at $2.05 billion, above an estimated $1.86 billion.

Get updates to this developing story directly on Stocktwits.

data.title: Uranium Energy Commences Production at Burke Hollow, New U.S. ISR Mine
data.description:
Uranium Energy commenced production at Burke Hollow after TCEQ approval, marking the newest U.S. ISR uranium mine and expanding its domestic production footprint.

Key Highlights:

  • Received Texas Commission on Environmental Quality approval and started production at Burke Hollow ISR project.
  • Burke Hollow is the world’s newest operating ISR uranium mine and the first new U.S. ISR operation in over a decade.
  • UEC now operates two active U.S. ISR hub-and-spoke platforms following recent Christensen Ranch capacity approvals.
  • Burke Hollow sits on ~20,000 acres with only half explored, supporting phased resource expansion and additional wellfields.
  • Production from Burke Hollow will be processed at Hobson Central Processing Plant, licensed up to 4 million lbs/year.

Original SEC Filing: URANIUM ENERGY CORP [ UEC ] - 8-K - Apr. 08, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Perma-Fix to Present April 2026 Investor Deck at Gabelli Waste & Environmental Symposium
data.description:
Perma-Fix will present its April 2026 investor presentation at the Gabelli Funds Waste & Environmental Services Symposium.

Key Highlights:

  • CEO and CFO to present at Gabelli Funds’ 12th Annual Waste & Environmental Services Symposium on April 9, 2026.
  • Attendees will be presented with the company’s "Investor Presentation, April 2026" (Exhibit 99.1).
  • The investor presentation is incorporated by reference into the Form 8-K and will be posted to the company website.

Original SEC Filing: PERMA FIX ENVIRONMENTAL SERVICES INC [ PESI ] - 8-K - Apr. 08, 2026

data.title: Inno Holdings launches AI strategic plan to boost mobile-phone inspection, rating and pricing
data.description:
Inno Holdings launched an AI Strategic Initiative aimed at improving efficiency in its electronic devices trading business.

Key Highlights:

  • Announced launch of an AI Strategic Initiative to enhance operational efficiency in electronic devices trading.
  • Plans to develop AI tools for mobile phone quality inspection, rating and pricing via in-house R&D and outsourcing.
  • Envisions integration of real-time analytics into procurement, sales and quality workflows to speed decisions.
  • Initiative is in early planning; timing, scope and successful implementation are uncertain and not assured.

Original SEC Filing: INNO HOLDINGS INC. [ INHD ] - 8-K - Apr. 08, 2026

data.title: Catalyst Bancorp to Acquire Lakeside for $41.1 Million Cash, $19.58 Per Share
data.description:
Catalyst Bancorp signed a definitive Agreement and Plan of Share Exchange and Merger to acquire Lakeside Bancshares for $19.58 per share in cash, valuing the deal at approximately $41.1 million, subject to adjustments. The boards of both companies unanimously approved the transaction, which is expected to close in the third quarter of 2026 pending Lakeside shareholder and regulatory approvals. To support the closing, Catalyst also secured voting commitments from Lakeside directors and entered into non-compete agreements with them to protect the combined franchise post-merger.

Agreement 1: Catalyst Bancorp to Acquire Lakeside for $41.1 Million Cash, $19.58 Per Share

  • Agreement type: Agreement and Plan of Share Exchange and Merger
  • Counterparty: Lakeside Bancshares and Lakeside Bank
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 07 2026 / same
  • Reason: Expand Louisiana banking franchise and customer reach

Agreement 2: Catalyst Bancorp Secures Voting Support From Lakeside Directors for Merger

  • Agreement type: Voting and Support Agreements
  • Counterparty: Directors of Lakeside Bancshares and Lakeside Bank
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 07 2026 / same
  • Reason: Secure shareholder approval and expedite closing

Agreement 3: Catalyst Bancorp Enters Non-Compete Agreements With Lakeside Directors

  • Agreement type: Restrictive Covenant (Non-Compete) Agreements
  • Counterparty: Directors of Lakeside Bancshares and Lakeside Bank
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 07 2026 / same
  • Reason: Protect franchise value and customer relationships post-merger

Original SEC Filing: Catalyst Bancorp, Inc. [ CLST ] - 8-K - Apr. 08, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.
